name: Scott Soape & Associates, LLC
description_short: Commercial debt negotiator specializing in settlement and payment plan arrangements for businesses with past-due debts, judgments, and liens since 1997.
description_long:
Scott F. Soape & Associates, LLC has operated as a commercial debt resolution firm since 1997, founded by Scott Soape, a University of Texas graduate with over 30 years of financial services experience. Soape previously worked as a bank executive, taught banking courses at Austin Community College, and operated a successful financial consulting business before focusing full-time on commercial debt resolution. The firm is based in the Austin, Texas area and serves businesses nationwide.

The company negotiates settlements and manageable payment arrangements for businesses facing past-due debts, disputed debts, judgments, liens, and lawsuits. They position themselves as representing the debtor business—unlike collection agencies—and aim to secure the best possible discounts on claimed amounts, lowest interest rates, longest repayment terms, and smallest monthly payments. Clients can choose between lump-sum settlements or structured monthly payment plans. The company claims a near 100% success rate over its operational history.

Scott Soape & Associates distinguishes itself through contingency-based fee arrangements: clients pay only if settlements are negotiated to their satisfaction. If no acceptable settlement or payment plan is reached, there is no charge. Fees are customized based on case size and complexity and are discussed during free initial consultations before any negotiations begin. The firm emphasizes affordability as an alternative to traditional debt resolution methods and guarantees their fee structure.

This company is specifically designed for small to medium-sized businesses with commercial debt problems rather than consumers. While the website claims assistance to thousands of businesses and near 100% success rates, these claims are not independently verified. The firm explicitly states it is not a law firm and provides no legal advice, which is an important limitation for complex disputes requiring legal representation.
pros: Contingency-based fee structure: no payment if settlements don't meet client standards
Nationwide service availability since 1997 with claimed near 100% success rate
Represents debtors, not creditors, ensuring alignment with business client interests
Free initial consultations to review cases and fee arrangements
Founder has 30+ years of financial services experience including bank executive background
Negotiates multiple favorable terms: principal reduction, lower interest rates, extended payment periods
Customized fee structure based on individual case complexity rather than flat rates
cons: Not a law firm and explicitly cannot provide legal advice, limiting utility for litigation-heavy disputes
Success rate claims (near 100%) are not independently verified or substantiated
Limited transparent pricing information; fees must be discussed in consultation rather than published upfront
Exclusively serves commercial/business clients, not consumer or personal debt situations
No clear information about timeline for debt resolution or typical negotiation processes
best_for: Small to medium-sized business owners with multiple past-due commercial debts or payment disputes
Business operators facing liens, judgments, or threatened lawsuits from creditors
Company decision-makers seeking affordable debt negotiation without immediate legal intervention
diagnosis: Best for small-to-medium business owners with commercial debts, judgments, or liens seeking affordable settlement negotiation on a contingency basis. Primary caveat: this is not a law firm and cannot provide legal advice, so businesses facing complex litigation may need to supplement with legal counsel; success rates are claimed but not independently verified.
